description:
Kind is the compiled output of `veil build` for a single kind. It bundles
 every source file's contents and every hook's minified code into a
 single document, so rendering does not need access to the original
 filesystem. Compare with KindDefinition (config.proto), which is the
 hand-authored source-side shape that this is compiled from.

dependents.description:
Compiled per-consumer dependent entries (one per consumer kind that
 may depend on this resource). Carries bundled hook code plus the
 inlined params JSON Schema so render does not need access to the
 original kind directory.

items.description:
Dependent is the compiled form of a KindDefinition.dependents entry: each
 hook source is bundled, and the params JSON Schema is embedded inline so
 rendering does not need access to the original kind directory.

^(paramsSchema)$.description: Inlined contents of the params JSON Schema (raw JSON text).

hooks.description: Compiled, bundled dependent hooks in declaration order.

items.description:
Hook is a single hook bundled and minified from its TS/JS source, with
 all of its imports resolved and inlined.

content.description: Minified JavaScript bundle, ES module format.

name.description: Relative path from the kind directory (e.g. "hooks/hello-world.ts").

items.title: Hook

kind.description:
The consumer kind that may depend on this resource (matches
 DependentDefinition.kind).

params_schema.description: Inlined contents of the params JSON Schema (raw JSON text).

items.title: Dependent

hooks.description:
Compiled hooks grouped by lifecycle point (matches
 KindDefinition.hooks).

render.description: Compiled hooks that run during `veil render`, in declaration order.

items.description:
Hook is a single hook bundled and minified from its TS/JS source, with
 all of its imports resolved and inlined.

content.description: Minified JavaScript bundle, ES module format.

name.description: Relative path from the kind directory (e.g. "hooks/hello-world.ts").

items.title: Hook

hooks.title: Hooks

sources.description:
Source files keyed by path relative to the kind directory. Values are
 the raw file contents.

additionalProperties.description:
Variable declares a named input available to overlay CEL expressions.
 Lives in both the local VeilConfigDefinition and the published Kind
 (carried verbatim through compilation), so it has no Definition
 suffix — the same shape applies in both contexts.

default.description:
Optional default. If omitted, the variable is required at render time.
 Must be a scalar matching the declared type.
default.title: Value

description.description:
Optional human-readable description. Rendered as a JSDoc comment on
 the field in the generated veil-types.ts RegistryVariables interface
 so hook authors get inline documentation at their IDE cursor.

enum.description:
Optional set of permitted values. Only valid for `string` and `number`
 types. When present, the generated TypeScript field becomes a union
 ("a" | "b" | "c") instead of the broad scalar type, and values
 supplied via --var or VEIL_VAR_* are rejected at render time unless
 they match one of the listed entries.

type.description:
Declared type. Must be one of the legal VariableType.Enum values
 (the `unspecified` zero is rejected at load time).
type.title: Enum

additionalProperties.title: Variable

variables.description:
Input variable declarations copied from veil.json. Consumers resolve
 values from --var flags, VEIL_VAR_* env vars, or the declared default.

title: Kind
